description: Google Workspace (formerly G Suite) security patterns: OAuth tokens, service-account JSON keys, Admin SDK misuse, and admin-console red flags. Applies to Gmail, Drive, Calendar, Meet, Docs/Sheets, Admin SDK, GAM, and Cloud Identity.

token_patterns.name: Google Service Account JSON (private_key block)
token_patterns.notes: Detects pasted Google service-account JSON. Domain-wide-delegation enabled service accounts are catastrophic if leaked — they can impersonate any Workspace user.

token_patterns.name: Google Cloud Storage Signed URL credential

misconfigurations.title: Drive 'Anyone with the link' must not be the default for the org
misconfigurations.rationale: Default-public link-sharing makes every accidentally-shared doc world-readable. GWS supports a domain-restricted default at the org-unit level.
misconfigurations.fix: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Drive and Docs > Sharing settings — set default to 'On - your-domain.com' and disable 'Anyone with the link'.

misconfigurations.title: Avoid `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/admin` super-admin OAuth scope
misconfigurations.rationale: Granting the full Admin SDK scope to a service account allows arbitrary user/group/role mutation. Domain-wide delegation amplifies this.
misconfigurations.fix: Replace with the narrowest specific scope you need — e.g. `admin.directory.user.readonly`, `admin.reports.audit.readonly`. Audit existing delegations in Admin Console > Security > API controls > Domain-wide Delegation.

misconfigurations.title: 2-Step Verification must be enforced for all users (and especially admins)
misconfigurations.rationale: Workspace admin compromises in the public record (e.g. 2023 Reddit, 2024 Snowflake-adjacent OAuth thefts) all involved accounts without 2SV or with SMS-fallback enabled.
misconfigurations.fix: Admin Console > Security > Authentication > 2-step verification — enforce, exclude only break-glass accounts which use hardware keys.

misconfigurations.title: No Context-Aware Access policy on Admin Console / Drive / Gmail
misconfigurations.rationale: Posture-based access controls (geo, device-state) materially reduce token-replay risk.
misconfigurations.fix: Admin Console > Security > Context-Aware Access — define IP/device-trust policies for Admin Console, Drive, Gmail at minimum.

misconfigurations.title: Service-account JSON keys must be rotated ≤ 90 days
misconfigurations.rationale: Long-lived service-account keys are the canonical GWS leak vector.
misconfigurations.fix: Prefer Workload Identity Federation. If keys are required, automate rotation via `gcloud iam service-accounts keys create/delete` on a 90-day schedule.

admin_red_flags.title: Data Export Tool (Vault) used by a non-IT principal
admin_red_flags.rationale: The Data Export Tool can extract an entire tenant's mail/Drive content. Legitimate use is rare.
admin_red_flags.signal: Admin Audit log: `DATA_EXPORT_CREATED` actor not in known IT-admin list.

admin_red_flags.title: Admin role granted to a non-standard account
admin_red_flags.rationale: Privilege escalation via role grant is a recurring GWS compromise pattern.
admin_red_flags.signal: Admin Audit log: `ASSIGN_ROLE` with role in {Super Admin, User Management Admin, Groups Admin} to an account not in the IT-admin group.

admin_red_flags.title: Sudden burst of `authorize` events for a previously-unseen OAuth app
admin_red_flags.rationale: OAuth phishing campaigns (e.g. consent phish) show up as many users granting the same brand-new client ID in a short window.
admin_red_flags.signal: Token activity report: distinct user count for a single client_id > 5 within 1h.

admin_red_flags.title: Domain-wide delegation added/modified for any service account
admin_red_flags.rationale: DWD is the most dangerous setting in Workspace. Every change should be reviewed.
admin_red_flags.signal: Admin Audit log: `AUTHORIZE_API_CLIENT_ACCESS` with scope list containing any `admin.directory.*` or `gmail.*`.

admin_red_flags.title: Automatic Gmail forwarding rule created to an external domain
admin_red_flags.rationale: Auto-forward to an attacker-controlled inbox is a classic BEC persistence.
